One Voice: Glorifying God through Our We: We turned to Romans 15:5-7 and saw that the church glorifies God with one voice by welcoming one another as Christ welcomed us. Because Jesus received us by mercy, we were called to repent of partiality, bear with one another in love, and make our life together a visible witness to the gospel.
One Voice: Glorifying God through Our Wo: We turned to Romans 15:1-13 and saw that God forms His church to glorify Him together with one voice. Because Christ did not please Himself but welcomed Jews and Gentiles into the hope of God's promises, we were called to worship with endurance, encouragement, harmony, and hope, bearing with one another for the glory of God.
The Short-Sighted Fool: We turned to Psalm 14 and saw the fool's refusal to reckon with God, a corruption that exposes every human heart's need. Because salvation comes from Zion through Christ, who alone is righteous, we were warned against practical unbelief and invited to find refuge and glad hope in the Lord.
A Life of Deep Faith: We turned to Psalm 13 and learned to bring our sorrow and unanswered questions honestly before the Lord. Because His steadfast love and salvation are sure in Christ, deep faith does not deny grief but carries it to God, asks Him to light our eyes, and learns to sing because He has dealt bountifully with us.
The Faithful and The Wicked: We turned to Psalm 12 and heard the cry, Save, O Lord, as the faithful seem to vanish and deceit fills the world. Because the Lord's words are pure and His promises are trustworthy, we were called to depend on Him, resist flattering lips, and hope in the God who guards His people from this generation forever.
The Holiness of Hunkerin': We turned to Psalm 11 and saw that when the foundations seem destroyed, the righteous take refuge in the Lord. Because He is in His holy temple, rules from His heavenly throne, and will judge wickedness while preserving His people, we were called to hunker down in faith, stand firm, and look toward the day we behold His face.
An Attentive God in Troubling Times: We turned to Psalm 10 and voiced the anguish of troubling times, when God seems far and the wicked prosper. Because the Lord sees, hears, and will arise to defend the helpless, we were invited to pray honestly, trust His justice, and entrust the vulnerable to the King who sets all things right.
Defiant Praise: We turned to Psalm 9 and heard defiant praise rising in the face of enemies, injustice, and affliction. Because the Lord reigns forever, judges with righteousness, and does not forget the afflicted, we were called to recount His deeds, trust His name, and praise Him as the stronghold who lifts His people.
Strength Through Weakness: We turned to Psalm 8 and saw the Lord's majesty displayed through the weakness of human creatures and the glory of creation. Because Jesus, the true Son of Man, was crowned with glory through suffering and reigns over all, we were humbled to trust God's power made visible through weakness.
